info.name: Vesicash API — Merchants Integration
info.description:
## Vesicash Portal API

**Base URL:** `https://api.vesicash.com`  
**API Version:** v1  
**Protocol:** HTTPS only

---

### Quick Start

1. Open the **Variables** tab and fill in:
   - `base_url` — sandbox or production base URL
   - `secret_key` — your Vesicash secret key
   - `public_key` — your Vesicash public key
   - `webhook_url` — your server endpoint for Vesicash events
   - `idempotency_key` — unique string per POST request

2. Use **test keys** for development. Switch to **live keys** after KYC approval.

3. All requests return a standard JSON envelope:

```json
{
  "status": "success" | "error",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Human-readable message",
  "data": { ... }
}
```

---

### Authentication

Every request requires these headers:

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `x-api-key` | Your secret API key — keep server-side only |
| `public-key` | Your public API key |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` |
| `Idempotency-Key` | Unique string per POST request |

---

### Failure Reason Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `ABANDONED_TRANSACTION` | PIN not entered in time |
| `DECLINED_TRANSACTION` | Rejected by processor |
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Customer has insufficient funds |
| `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` | Payment provider timed out |
| `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` | Recipient account does not exist |
| `DUPLICATE_REFERENCE` | Idempotency or transaction reference already used |
| `INVALID_CARD` | Card failed validation |
| `3DS_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED` | 3D Secure authentication failed |

item.name: Getting Started
item.description:
## Getting Started

Welcome to the Vesicash Merchant API. This section covers authentication, environments, and your first API call.

### Before you begin

1. Sign up at [vesicash.com](https://vesicash.com) and complete KYC.
2. Go to **Dashboard → Developer → API Keys** to generate your test and live keys.
3. Set the collection variables:
   - `base_url`
   - `secret_key`
   - `public_key`
   - `webhook_url`
   - `idempotency_key`

### Environments

| Environment | Base URL | Key prefix |
|-------------|----------|------------|
| Sandbox | `https://sandbox.api.vesicash.com` | `sk_test_`, `pk_test_` |
| Production | `https://api.vesicash.com` | `sk_live_`, `pk_live_` |

> ✅ **Tip:** Always test thoroughly in sandbox before switching to live keys.

request.description:
---
docs-type: guide
---

## Introduction

### Before you begin
- Create a free account at [portal.vesicash.com](https://portal.vesicash.com) and verify your business.
- Copy your **Secret Key** from the API Keys section of your dashboard.
- Use your **test key** while building — test transactions never touch real funds.

## API basics

The Vesicash Merchant API is a REST API that gives you programmatic access to payments, payouts, and transaction management across Africa. Every feature in the Vesicash Portal Dashboard is available via the API, so you can build fully custom payment experiences from the ground up.

All requests are made over **HTTPS** and encrypted with TLS. Plain HTTP requests are rejected.

## Base URL

All API requests — both test and live — use the same base URL. The environment is determined entirely by the API key you supply:

```@code title="API BASE URL" hideCopy class='mb-12'
https://api.vesicash.com
```

## Authentication

The Vesicash API uses API keys to authenticate requests. Pass your **Secret Key** in the `x-api-key` header. Find your key in the [Vesicash Dashboard](https://portal.vesicash.com) under Settings → API Keys.

```@code title="Authorization Header" language="bash" hideCopy
x-api-key: YOUR_SECRET_KEY
```

## HTTP methods

The Vesicash Portal API follows REST conventions:

| Method | Operation | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `POST` | Create | Creates a resource or triggers an action on the server |
| `GET` | Read | Retrieves an existing resource or a list of resources |
| `PUT` | Replace | Replaces an existing resource in full |
| `PATCH` | Update | Updates specific fields of an existing resource |
| `DELETE` | Remove | Removes a resource |

## Making requests

Requests must always include your `x-api-key` header. When sending a body, set `Content-Type: application/json`.

```@code title="Example POST Request" language="bash"
POST {{API_BASE_URL}}/v1/payment/init
x-api-key: sk_test_xxxxxxxxxxxx
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "amount": 5000,
  "country": "GH",
  "currency": "GHS",
  "method": "card",
  "operator": "mpgs",
  "narration": "Payment for Order #1234"
}
```

For `GET` requests, pass parameters directly in the query string:

```@code title="Example GET Request" language="bash" hideCopy
GET {{API_BASE_URL}}/v1/payment?page=1&limit=20
x-api-key: sk_test_xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

## Response format

Every response from the Vesicash Portal API follows the same envelope structure:

```@code title="Success Response" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": { ... }
}
```

When a request fails, the same envelope is returned with `"status": "error"`:

```@code title="Error Response" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "error",
  "code": 400,
  "message": "The amount field is required.",
  "data": null
}
```

For list endpoints, `data` includes both the resource array and a `meta` object:

```@code title="Paginated Response" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "transactions": [ ... ],
    "meta": {
      "page": 1,
      "totalPages": 8,
      "perPage": 20,
      "total": 143,
      "next": "/v1/payment?page=2&limit=20",
      "previous": null
    }
  }
}
```

`meta.next` and `meta.previous` are fully-formed URL paths you can use directly. When on the last page, `meta.next` is `null`.

---
docs-type: guide
---

## Authentication

All requests to the Vesicash API must be authenticated. Authentication identifies which integration a request belongs to and determines whether it is authorised to perform the requested action.

## API Keys

Every Vesicash merchant account gets two types of API keys:

| Type | Prefix | Use |
|------|--------|-----|
| Public Key | `VCpub_` | Frontend environments (mobile SDK, web). Can only initiate transactions and read non-sensitive data. |
| Secret Key | `VCsec_` | Backend server code only. Grants full account access. Never expose in client-side code, mobile apps, or public repositories. |

Find your API keys in **Settings → API Keys** on the [Vesicash Dashboard](https://portal.vesicash.com).

## Test and Live Modes

Vesicash provides two environments for every integration:

**Test mode** is for development and integration testing. Transactions do not involve real funds. Test keys carry a `test_` infix — e.g. `VCsec_test_xxxxxxxxxxxx`.

**Live mode** processes real transactions and moves actual funds. Live keys carry a `live_` infix — e.g. `VCsec_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx`.

```@callout type="tip" title="Tip"
Complete your full integration and QA cycle in test mode before switching to live keys. Both modes share the same base URL and API surface.
```

## Using Your Keys

Pass your Secret Key in the `Authorization` header:

```@code title="Authorization Header" language="bash" hideCopy
Authorization: Bearer VCsec_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

Some endpoints instead use the `x-api-key` header:

```@code title="x-api-key Header" language="bash" hideCopy
x-api-key: VCsec_live_xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

Each endpoint's reference page specifies which header to use.

## Sample Authentication Header

```@code title="Sample Header" language="bash" hideCopy
Authorization: Bearer VCsec_test_3k9mXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
```

API requests without a valid authentication header will fail with status `401 Unauthorised`. All requests must be made over **HTTPS** — plain HTTP is rejected.

## Key Management Best Practices

- **Never commit your secret key** to version control, mobile app code, or client-side JavaScript.
- **Store keys using environment variables** or a dedicated secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, etc.).
- **Rotate keys periodically** — especially if you suspect compromise. Regenerate from the dashboard at any time; previously issued keys are invalidated immediately.
- **Use separate keys per environment** — maintain distinct test and live key sets to prevent accidental live charges during development or CI.

---
docs-type: guide
---

## Rate Limits

Rate limiting is one of the mechanisms Vesicash uses to protect the stability and availability of the API. It prevents any single integration from monopolising shared infrastructure.

When you exceed a rate limit, the API returns `429 Too Many Requests`. The response includes a `Retry-After` header indicating how long to wait before resuming.

## Rate Limit Headers

Vesicash includes the following headers on every API response so you can track your consumption in real time:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | Maximum number of requests allowed in the current window |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | Requests remaining in the current rate window |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | Unix timestamp at which the current window resets |

## Standard Limits

Most endpoints share the following default limit:

| Mode | Limit |
|------|-------|
| Live | 100 requests per 60 seconds |
| Test | 100 requests per 60 seconds |

```@callout type="warning" title="Heads up"
Test mode is designed for integration testing, not load testing. Do not run performance or stress tests against the Vesicash test environment.
```

## Handling a 429

When an endpoint returns `429 Too Many Requests`, your integration should follow these steps:

```@step number=1 title="Read the Retry-After header"
The `Retry-After` response header contains the number of seconds to wait before resuming requests.
```

```@step number=2 title="Stop sending new requests"
Do not queue or fire additional requests while backing off. Check `X-RateLimit-Remaining` to track your current allowance.
```

```@step number=3 title="Wait, then retry" last=true
Wait the exact duration from `Retry-After`, then resume. Adapt your cadence dynamically using `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and `X-RateLimit-Reset` — avoid fixed-frequency polling.
```

## Staying Within Limits

A few patterns that help you stay well within rate limits in production:

- **Listen for webhooks instead of polling.** Register a `webhook_url` on your transaction requests. Vesicash will POST a status update the moment a payment changes state — no polling loop needed.
- **Cache stable responses.** Resources like country lists and bank lists rarely change. Fetch them once on startup, cache locally, and refresh on a schedule.
- **Use list endpoints with pagination.** Fetching individual transactions in a loop counts each call against your limit. Use `GET /v1/payment` with `page` and `limit` to retrieve multiple records per request.
- **Back off exponentially on retries.** For transient `5xx` errors, retry with exponential backoff: wait 1s, then 2s, 4s, 8s — give up after 4–5 attempts.

---
docs-type: guide
---

## Pagination

Endpoints that return collections of resources — transactions, payouts, wallets — support pagination. The Vesicash API uses **offset-based pagination** on all list endpoints.

## Pagination Parameters

Pass these query parameters to any list endpoint:

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | `1` | The page number to retrieve. Starts at `1`. |
| `limit` | integer | `20` | Number of results to return per page. Maximum is `100`. |

```@code title="Example Request" language="bash" hideCopy
GET /v1/payment?page=2&limit=50
```

## Pagination Response

Every list response wraps results in a consistent `data` envelope:

```@code title="Paginated Response" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "transactions": [ ... ],
    "pagination": {
      "page": 2,
      "totalPages": 8,
      "limit": 50,
      "total": 371
    }
  }
}
```

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `page` | The current page number |
| `totalPages` | Total number of pages given the current `limit` |
| `limit` | Number of results returned per page |
| `total` | Total number of matching records across all pages |

## Filtering Alongside Pagination

List endpoints accept filter parameters that combine with `page` and `limit`:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `from` | integer | Unix timestamp — include only records created on or after this time |
| `to` | integer | Unix timestamp — include only records created on or before this time |
| `reference` | string | Return the single transaction matching this Vesicash reference |

```@code title="Date-filtered Request" language="bash" hideCopy
GET /v1/payment?from=1700000000&to=1700086400&page=1&limit=20
```

## Best Practices

```@callout type="tip" title="Start with a small page size"
Don't default to `limit=100`. Start smaller and increase only when your pipeline can handle it — smaller pages respond faster and are easier to process incrementally.
```

- **Use `total` from page 1 to plan ahead.** The first response tells you how many records and pages exist. Fetch subsequent pages only if you need them.
- **Always paginate in production.** Fetching all records in a single call with a very high `limit` puts unnecessary load on both your system and the API. Process records in batches.
- **Scope queries with date filters.** Use `from` and `to` to retrieve records for the specific period you need — this dramatically reduces both response time and page count.

---
docs-type: guide
---

## Errors

When a request to the Vesicash API cannot be completed, the API returns an error response. All errors follow a consistent JSON structure designed to be easy to parse and handle programmatically.

## HTTP Status Codes

Vesicash uses standard HTTP status codes to indicate the outcome of every request:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `200` | **OK** — The request succeeded. |
| `201` | **Created** — A new resource was created successfully. |
| `400` | **Bad Request** — The request is malformed or missing required parameters. Check your request body and query string. |
| `401` | **Unauthorised** — Your API key is missing, invalid, or has been revoked. Re-check your `Authorization` header. |
| `403` | **Forbidden** — Your key is valid but lacks permission for this action (e.g. using a public key where a secret key is required). |
| `404` | **Not Found** — The requested resource does not exist. Check the reference or ID you supplied. |
| `422` | **Unprocessable Entity** — The request is well-formed but the values fail validation (e.g. an unsupported currency code). |
| `429` | **Too Many Requests** — You have exceeded the rate limit. Read the `Retry-After` header and back off before retrying. |
| `500` | **Server Error** — Something went wrong on Vesicash's end. These are rare and are monitored automatically. |

## Error Response Format

Every error response uses the same envelope as a success response, with `"status": "error"`:

```@code title="Error Response" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "error",
  "code": 400,
  "message": "The amount field is required.",
  "data": null
}
```

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `status` | Always `"error"` for failed requests |
| `code` | The HTTP status code, mirrored in the body for convenience |
| `message` | A human-readable description of what went wrong |
| `data` | `null` for most errors; may contain field-level validation detail for `422` responses |

## Validation Errors

For `400` and `422` responses, the `data` field may contain a map of field-level errors:

```@code title="Validation Error" language="json" hideCopy
{
  "status": "error",
  "code": 422,
  "message": "Validation failed",
  "data": {
    "amount": ["Amount must be a positive integer."],
    "currency": ["Currency code 'XYZ' is not supported."]
  }
}
```

Each key in `data` corresponds to the name of the failing field; each value is an array of error strings explaining what went wrong.

## Error Categories

Vesicash errors fall into three broad categories:

| Category | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `api_error` | Something went wrong on Vesicash's infrastructure. Retry with exponential backoff. |
| `validation_error` | A required field is missing, the wrong type, or fails a constraint. Fix the request before retrying. |
| `processing_error` | The request was valid but the payment processor or banking partner declined it (e.g. insufficient funds, card blocked). Do not auto-retry without customer action. |

## Error Handling Best Practices

```@callout type="warning" title="Always check status in the response body"
Never rely solely on the HTTP status code. Some gateways or proxies may return `200` with an error body — always read `status` from the response envelope to confirm the outcome.
```

- **Show `message` to your support team, not to end users.** Error messages are technical. Display your own user-friendly copy in your UI.
- **Log `code` + `message` + the request reference together** to make errors searchable in your logging system.
- **Don't auto-retry `4xx` errors.** Client errors mean the request itself is wrong — retrying the same request produces the same error.
- **Retry `5xx` errors with exponential backoff.** Server errors are transient. Wait 1s, then 2s, 4s, 8s — give up after 4–5 attempts.

---
docs-type: guide
---

## Accept Payments Overview

### In a nutshell
Accept mobile money and card payments from customers across Africa. Choose between a Vesicash-hosted checkout page or a direct integration built into your own UI.

### Before you begin
- You have completed KYC and generated API keys.
- You have set your `secret_key` and `public_key` in your environment.
- You have configured a `webhook_url` to receive event notifications.

## Hosted Checkout Flow

The simplest integration. Vesicash collects payment details on a hosted page — no card data touches your server.

```@step number=1 title="Initialize the payment"
Call `POST /v1/payment/init`. Vesicash returns a `payment_link` and a `reference`.
```

```@step number=2 title="Redirect the customer"
Send the customer to `data.payment_link`. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 entirely.
```

```@step number=3 title="Handle the redirect"
After payment, Vesicash redirects to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url` with `?payment_reference=<ref>` appended.
```

```@step number=4 title="Verify the outcome" last=true
**Always** confirm with `GET /v1/payment/:reference` before fulfilling the order. Never rely on redirect parameters alone.
```

## Direct Integration Flow

Build your own payment UI. You collect card details and submit them directly to Vesicash.

```@step number=1 title="Initialize the payment"
Call `POST /v1/payment/init` with `integration_type: "DIRECT_API"`. You receive a `reference` — no `payment_link` is returned.
```

```@step number=2 title="Collect and submit card details"
POST the customer's card details and device fingerprint to `POST /v1/payment/pay/:reference`.
```

```@step number=3 title="Handle 3DS2 challenge (if required)"
If the issuer returns `challenge_required`, display the ACS challenge to the customer and await the webhook confirmation.
```

```@step number=4 title="Verify the outcome" last=true
Call `GET /v1/payment/:reference` to confirm `status: "success"` before fulfilling the order.
```

## Supported Currencies

| Currency | Payment methods |
|----------|-----------------|
| `NGN` | card, bank_transfer, ussd |
| `GHS` | card, mobile_money |
| `KES` | card, mobile_money |
| `ZMW` | card, mobile_money |
| `ZAR` | card, bank_transfer |

## Related Flows
- [Mobile Money — Checkout](/accept-payments/mobile-money-checkout/initialize-payment)
- [Mobile Money — Direct](/accept-payments/mobile-money-direct/initialize-payment)
- [Card — Checkout](/accept-payments/card-checkout/initialize-payment)
- [Card — Direct](/accept-payments/card-direct/initialize-payment)

item.name: Mobile Money — Checkout
item.description:
## Mobile Money — Checkout

### In a nutshell
Host the payment experience on a Vesicash checkout page. This is the simplest integration and keeps your PCI scope minimal.

### When to use
- You want the fastest integration.
- You prefer Vesicash to handle payment details and provider redirects.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/init
2. Vesicash      →  Returns payment_link + reference
3. Your frontend →  Redirects customer to payment_link
4. Customer      →  Completes payment on Vesicash page
5. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
6. Your backend  →  GET /v1/payment/:reference (verify status)
```

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Never confirm an order based on the redirect URL alone. Always verify status via the API.

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Creates a new card payment transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted `payment_link` to which you redirect the customer. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 entirely.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | The transaction amount in the **smallest currency unit**. For Ghana, pass pesewas (1 GHS = 100 pesewas); for Nigeria, pass kobo (1 NGN = 100 kobo); for Zambia, pass ngwe (1 ZMW = 100 ngwe). Must be a positive integer. |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the transaction. Determines which banking network Vesicash routes through. Example: `GH` for Ghana, `NG` for Nigeria, `ZM` for Zambia, `KE` for Kenya. |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the country you specified. Example: `GHS` for Ghana Cedis, `NGN` for Nigerian Naira, `ZMW` for Zambian Kwacha. |
| `method` | string | ✅ | The payment channel to use. Set to `"card"` to process a card payment through the Mastercard Payment Gateway. |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | The payment processor to route through. Use `"mpgs"` for Mastercard Payment Gateway Services, which supports 3DS2 card processing across all supported markets. |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | A short description of what the customer is paying for. Displayed on the Vesicash checkout page and included on the customer's bank statement. Maximum 100 characters. |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here after a successful payment, appending `?payment_reference=<ref>` as a query parameter. Must be HTTPS in production. |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here if the payment fails or is abandoned. Use this to show an appropriate retry or support message. |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload to this URL whenever the transaction status changes. Always verify the signature using your webhook secret. |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | Defines the integration mode. Set to `"DIRECT_API"` for all server-to-server integrations. This controls whether Vesicash uses a hosted checkout page or returns raw payment data for your own UI. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Payment initialized",
  "data": {
    "payment_link": "https://checkout.vesicash.com/pay/abc123",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

---

### Next Step
Redirect the customer to `data.payment_link`. After payment, Vesicash will redirect back to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url`. **Always verify the outcome using `GET /v1/payment/:reference`.**

## Verify Payment Status (Post-Redirect)

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Call this after the customer returns to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url` — or upon receiving a webhook event — to confirm the authoritative payment outcome.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` returned by `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated from the previous step's test script. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "environment": "live",
    "redirect_url": "https://yourbank.com/payment/success",
    "email": "customer@email.com",
    "customer_first_name": "John",
    "customer_last_name": "Doe",
    "phone_number": "+233XXXXXXXXX"
  }
}
```

---

> ⚠️ **Critical:** Never finalise an order, credit a wallet, or trigger fulfilment based on the redirect URL params alone. Always call this endpoint to confirm `status: "success"` before taking business action.

item.name: Mobile Money — Direct
item.description:
## Mobile Money — Direct

### In a nutshell
Collect mobile money payments directly in your own UI without redirecting to Vesicash.

### When to use
- You want full control over the customer experience.
- You have the compliance and UI resources to collect payment details yourself.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/init
2. Vesicash      →  Returns payment_reference
3. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/pay/:reference (customer details)
4. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
5. Your backend  →  GET /v1/payment/:reference (verify status)
```

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Always verify the final status via the API before fulfilling an order.

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Non-Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Same endpoint as the checkout flow, but returns a `payment_reference` (not a `payment_link`) which you use in the next step to submit card details directly.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | The transaction amount in the **smallest currency unit**. For Ghana, pass pesewas (1 GHS = 100 pesewas); for Nigeria, pass kobo (1 NGN = 100 kobo); for Zambia, pass ngwe (1 ZMW = 100 ngwe). Must be a positive integer. |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the transaction. Determines which banking network Vesicash routes through. Example: `GH` for Ghana, `NG` for Nigeria, `ZM` for Zambia, `KE` for Kenya. |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the country you specified. Example: `GHS` for Ghana Cedis, `NGN` for Nigerian Naira, `ZMW` for Zambian Kwacha. |
| `method` | string | ✅ | The payment channel to use. Set to `"card"` to process a card payment through the Mastercard Payment Gateway. |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | The payment processor to route through. Use `"mpgs"` for Mastercard Payment Gateway Services, which supports 3DS2 card processing across all supported markets. |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | A short description of what the customer is paying for. Displayed on the Vesicash checkout page and included on the customer's bank statement. Maximum 100 characters. |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here after a successful payment, appending `?payment_reference=<ref>` as a query parameter. Must be HTTPS in production. |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here if the payment fails or is abandoned. Use this to show an appropriate retry or support message. |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload to this URL whenever the transaction status changes. Always verify the signature using your webhook secret. |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | Defines the integration mode. Set to `"DIRECT_API"` for all server-to-server integrations. This controls whether Vesicash uses a hosted checkout page or returns raw payment data for your own UI. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

**Note:** In the non-checkout flow, `data.reference` is the `payment_reference` you pass to the next step. There is no `payment_link`.

request.description:
## Complete Payment — Submit Card Data

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/pay/:payment_reference`

Submit the customer's card details and device fingerprint to complete the payment initiated in the previous step. Vesicash processes the card through the MPGS network and triggers 3DS2 if required.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated. |

---

### Request Body

**Customer Identity:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `phone` | string | ✅ | Customer's phone number in E.164 international format, e.g. `+233501234567`. Used for 3DS2 authentication SMS and receipt delivery. |
| `email` | string | ✅ | Customer's email address. Used for 3DS2 authentication and to send the payment receipt. Must be a valid RFC 5321 address. |
| `name` | string | ✅ | Customer's full name as it appears on their account. Displayed on the receipt and used for fraud-screening checks. |

**Card Details:**

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `card.number` | string | ✅ | The Primary Account Number (PAN) — 16-digit card number, no spaces or dashes. Transmitted over TLS; Vesicash does not store raw PANs. |
| `card.expiry` | string | ✅ | Card expiry in `MM/YY` format, e.g. `07/26`. Must match the date embossed on the card. |
| `card.cvv` | string | ✅ | The Card Verification Value printed on the card (3 digits for Visa/Mastercard, 4 for Amex). Never stored after authorisation. |

**Device Fingerprint (all required for 3DS2 risk scoring):**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `device.screen_width` | integer | The customer's screen width in pixels (`screen.width`). Collected client-side and required for 3DS2 device profiling. |
| `device.screen_height` | integer | The customer's screen height in pixels (`screen.height`). Collected client-side and required for 3DS2 device profiling. |
| `device.browser_agent` | string | The full `navigator.userAgent` string from the customer's browser. Used by MPGS for 3DS2 risk-based authentication. |
| `device.user_ip` | string | Customer's public IP — use `GET /v1/decision/ip` from browser |
| `device.time_zone` | string | The customer's browser timezone as an IANA name, e.g. `Africa/Accra`. Obtain via `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone`. |
| `device.java_enabled` | boolean | Whether Java is enabled in the browser. Obtain via `navigator.javaEnabled()`. Pass `false` for modern browsers which disable Java by default. |
| `device.language` | string | The browser's preferred language tag, e.g. `en-GB`. Obtain via `navigator.language`. Part of the 3DS2 device fingerprint. |

---

### 3DS2 Challenge Response

If the issuer requires a challenge, Vesicash returns:

```json
{
  "status": "challenge_required",
  "data": {
    "device_storage_token": "...",
    "methodUrl": "https://issuer-acs.example.com/3ds/method",
    "methodPostData": "<base64-encoded-payload>"
  }
}
```

**Your bank frontend must:**
1. POST `methodPostData` to `methodUrl` (Card Issuer ACS)
2. Display the issuer's challenge to the customer (OTP / in-app / biometric)
3. On completion, Vesicash will deliver the final result via webhook

---

### Success Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "status": "success",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX"
  }
}
```

## Verify Transaction Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Returns the full transaction record including all fields relevant for audit, refund eligibility and fraud monitoring.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` from the init step. Auto-populated. |

---

### Response (key fields)

```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "charge": 150,
    "environment": "live",
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "narration": "Payment for Order #12345",
    "provider_transaction_id": "MPGS-XXXXXX",
    "operator": "mpgs",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "is_disbursed": false,
    "is_fraud_resolved": false,
    "refunds": [],
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:05Z"
  }
}
```

---

### Fields to monitor

| Field | What to watch for |
|-------|------------------|
| `status` | `"success"` before confirming order |
| `is_fraud_resolved` | `false` = still under fraud review |
| `failure_reason` | Present on failed transactions |
| `refunds` | Array grows as refunds are processed |

item.name: Card — Checkout
item.description:
## Card — Checkout

### In a nutshell
Accept card payments through a Vesicash-hosted checkout page. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 authentication.

### When to use
- You want the simplest card integration.
- You want to minimize PCI-DSS scope.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/init
2. Vesicash      →  Returns payment_link + reference
3. Your frontend →  Redirects customer to payment_link
4. Customer      →  Enters card details on Vesicash page
5. Vesicash      →  Handles 3DS2 with card issuer
6. Vesicash      →  Redirects customer back to your site
7. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
8. Your backend  →  GET /v1/payment/:reference (verify status)
```

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Never confirm an order based on the redirect URL alone. Always verify status via the API.

request.description:
## Initialize Payment — Checkout Flow

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/init`

Creates a new card payment transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted `payment_link` to which you redirect the customer. Vesicash handles card data collection and 3DS2 entirely.

---

### Request Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | ✅ | The transaction amount in the **smallest currency unit**. For Ghana, pass pesewas (1 GHS = 100 pesewas); for Nigeria, pass kobo (1 NGN = 100 kobo); for Zambia, pass ngwe (1 ZMW = 100 ngwe). Must be a positive integer. |
| `country` | string | ✅ | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the transaction. Determines which banking network Vesicash routes through. Example: `GH` for Ghana, `NG` for Nigeria, `ZM` for Zambia, `KE` for Kenya. |
| `currency` | string | ✅ | ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the country you specified. Example: `GHS` for Ghana Cedis, `NGN` for Nigerian Naira, `ZMW` for Zambian Kwacha. |
| `method` | string | ✅ | The payment channel to use. Set to `"card"` to process a card payment through the Mastercard Payment Gateway. |
| `operator` | string | ✅ | The payment processor to route through. Use `"mpgs"` for Mastercard Payment Gateway Services, which supports 3DS2 card processing across all supported markets. |
| `narration` | string | ✅ | A short description of what the customer is paying for. Displayed on the Vesicash checkout page and included on the customer's bank statement. Maximum 100 characters. |
| `redirect_success_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here after a successful payment, appending `?payment_reference=<ref>` as a query parameter. Must be HTTPS in production. |
| `redirect_failed_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here if the payment fails or is abandoned. Use this to show an appropriate retry or support message. |
| `webhook_url` | string | ✅ | A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload to this URL whenever the transaction status changes. Always verify the signature using your webhook secret. |
| `integration_type` | string | ✅ | Defines the integration mode. Set to `"DIRECT_API"` for all server-to-server integrations. This controls whether Vesicash uses a hosted checkout page or returns raw payment data for your own UI. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Payment initialized",
  "data": {
    "payment_link": "https://checkout.vesicash.com/pay/abc123",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "payment_id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending"
  }
}
```

---

### Next Step
Redirect the customer to `data.payment_link`. After payment, Vesicash will redirect back to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url`. **Always verify the outcome using `GET /v1/payment/:reference`.**

## Verify Payment Status (Post-Redirect)

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Call this after the customer returns to your `redirect_success_url` or `redirect_failed_url` — or upon receiving a webhook event — to confirm the authoritative payment outcome.

---

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `payment_reference` | The `reference` returned by `POST /v1/payment/init`. Auto-populated from the previous step's test script. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "id": "pay_XXXXXX",
    "amount": 10000,
    "type": "payment",
    "method": "card",
    "status": "success",
    "currency": "GHS",
    "reference": "APX-REF-XXXXXX",
    "business_id": "biz_XXX",
    "created_at": "2026-05-25T10:00:00Z",
    "environment": "live",
    "redirect_url": "https://yourbank.com/payment/success",
    "email": "customer@email.com",
    "customer_first_name": "John",
    "customer_last_name": "Doe",
    "phone_number": "+233XXXXXXXXX"
  }
}
```

---

> ⚠️ **Critical:** Never finalise an order, credit a wallet, or trigger fulfilment based on the redirect URL params alone. Always call this endpoint to confirm `status: "success"` before taking business action.

## Card — Direct

### In a nutshell
Collect card details in your own UI and submit them directly to Vesicash. You maintain full control over the payment experience.

### When to use
- You want full control over the card UI.
- You are PCI-DSS compliant or use a certified card tokenization provider.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/init
2. Vesicash      →  Returns payment_reference
3. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payment/pay/:reference (card data + device fingerprint)
4. Vesicash      →  Returns success or 3DS2 challenge
5. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
6. Your backend  →  GET /v1/payment/:reference (verify status)
```

> ⚠️ **Warning:** Always verify the final status via the API before fulfilling an order.

request.description:
---
docs-type: guide
---

## Send Payouts Overview

### In a nutshell
Disburse funds to bank accounts and mobile money wallets across Africa.

### Before you begin
- Your merchant wallet has sufficient balance for the payout amount.
- You have collected accurate recipient details (account number, bank code, or mobile number).
- You have configured a `webhook_url` to receive payout status events.

## Payout Flow

```@step number=1 title="Check your wallet balance"
Call `GET /api/v1/merchant/wallets` and confirm `balance` is greater than or equal to the payout amount. Payouts fail immediately with `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` if your balance is too low.
```

```@step number=2 title="Initiate the payout"
Call `POST /v1/payment/payouts/process` with recipient details and the amount. Vesicash queues the payout asynchronously and returns a `reference`.
```

```@step number=3 title="Receive the webhook"
Vesicash POSTs a `payout.completed` or `payout.failed` event to your `webhook_url` when the transfer is processed.
```

```@step number=4 title="Verify the outcome" last=true
Call `GET /v1/payment/:reference` and confirm `is_disbursed: true` before updating your records.
```

## Payout Types

### Bank Payout
Send money to a bank account using the recipient's account number and bank code. Set `transfer_to: "bank"`.

### Mobile Money Payout
Send money directly to a mobile money wallet. Set `transfer_to: "mobile_money"`.

## Related Endpoints
- [Bank Payout](/send-payouts/bank-payout/process-payout)
- [Mobile Money Payout](/send-payouts/mobile-money-payout/process-payout)

item.name: Mobile Money Payout
item.description:
## Mobile Money Payout

### In a nutshell
Disburse funds directly to mobile money wallets across supported countries.

### Before you begin
- Ensure your merchant wallet has sufficient balance.
- Confirm the recipient's mobile money number and country.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  GET /v1/wallet/:country (check balance)
2. Your backend  →  POST /v1/payout
3. Vesicash      →  Processes payout asynchronously
4. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
5. Your backend  →  GET /v1/payout/:reference (verify status)
```

request.description:
## Check Wallet Balance

**Endpoint:** `GET /api/v1/merchant/wallets`

Returns all active wallets for your Vesicash account. **Always check your wallet balance before initiating a payout** — insufficient funds will cause the payout to fail immediately with `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`.

---

### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `uuid` | string | Wallet unique identifier |
| `country_code` | string | ISO country code of this wallet |
| `currency` | string | Currency code (e.g. `ZMW`, `GHS`) |
| `balance` | number | Available payout balance |
| `collection_balance` | number | Collected funds not yet settled |
| `status` | string | `"active"` or `"suspended"` |

---

> ✅ Ensure `balance` ≥ the payout amount before proceeding to Step 2.

request.description:
## Process Bank Payout

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/payouts/process`

Initiates a disbursement from your Vesicash wallet to a beneficiary's bank account.

---

### Request Body

**Required fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | integer | The payout amount in the **smallest currency unit** (e.g. pesewas for GHS, kobo for NGN). Must be a positive integer and cannot exceed the available balance in your wallet for that currency. |
| `countryId` | string | The destination country identifier. Retrieve the list of supported countries and their IDs from `GET /v1/countries`. This determines which banking network handles the transfer. |
| `transfer_to` | string | The payout channel. Set to `"bank"` to send funds directly to a bank account. Additional channels may be supported in future. |
| `bank_id` | string | The identifier of the beneficiary's bank. Retrieve available banks for a given country from `GET /v1/payment/banks`. Must correspond to a bank operating in the country specified by `countryId`. |
| `account_number` | string | The beneficiary's bank account number. Must be a valid account at the specified bank. Vesicash may perform a name lookup before disbursing funds. |

**Optional fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `narration` | string | A short text shown on the beneficiary's bank statement. Defaults to your business name if omitted. Maximum 100 characters. |
| `webhook_url` | string | A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload when the payout status changes (e.g. from `processing` to `completed` or `failed`). |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 0,
  "message": "Payout request accepted",
  "data": {
    "reference": "APX-PO-XXXXXX",
    "amount": 50000,
    "status": "processing"
  }
}
```

---

### Failure Codes Specific to Payouts

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Wallet balance too low — check with `GET /v1/account/get-wallet` |
| `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` | Invalid `account_number` or `bank_id` combination |
| `ACCOUNT_BLOCKED` | Beneficiary account is frozen |
| `TRANSACTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Amount exceeds configured payout limit |

## Check Payout Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Verifies the final outcome of a payout. Call after receiving a webhook notification, or poll periodically if webhooks are not configured.

---

### Key Response Fields for Payouts

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `status` | `"success"`, `"failed"`, or `"processing"` |
| `is_disbursed` | `true` when funds have been delivered to the beneficiary |
| `failure_reason` | Present if `status` is `"failed"` |
| `amount` | Disbursed amount |
| `reference` | Your payout reference |
| `created_at` | When the payout was initiated |
| `updated_at` | Last status change timestamp |

response.body:
{
    "success": true,
    "code": 200,
    "data": {
        "uuid": "1c543991-bdc3-4918-a800-6f19b61a0781",
        "reference": "PAYOUT-VES-1781276304",
        "type": "pay_out",
        "status": "completed",
        "method": "mobilemoney",
        "environment": "live",
        "amount": 0.3,
        "currency": "ZMW",
        "fee": 0,
        "net_amount": 0.3,
        "narration": "Payout to customer",
        "account_number": "260968832801",
        "is_disbursed": true,
        "failure_reason": "",
        "created_at": "2026-06-15T09:27:44Z",
        "completed_at": "2026-06-15T09:29:12Z"
    }
}

item.name: Process Refund
item.description:
## Process Refund

### In a nutshell
Reverse a completed payment back to the customer.

### Before you begin
- The original payment must have `status: "success"`.
- Your wallet must have sufficient balance to cover the refund amount.

### Flow
```
1. Your backend  →  POST /v1/refund
2. Vesicash      →  Processes refund asynchronously
3. Vesicash      →  POSTs webhook event to your server
4. Your backend  →  GET /v1/refund/:reference (verify status)
```

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Status: pending — wait for webhook or poll status.');

request.description:
## Process Refund

**Endpoint:** `POST /v1/payment/refunds/process`

Initiates a refund against a completed card payment. Vesicash queues the reversal and routes it through the card network back to the customer's card issuer.

---

### Request Body

**Required fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `amount` | number | The amount to refund in the **smallest currency unit** (e.g. pesewas for GHS). Must be greater than zero and cannot exceed the original transaction amount. Partial refunds are supported. |
| `payment_reference` | string | The Vesicash reference of the original successful payment you want to refund. Only transactions with status `"completed"` or `"success"` can be refunded. |
| `reason` | string | A short explanation for why this refund is being issued. Stored against the refund record for audit purposes and may appear in reporting. |
| `country_id` | string | The country identifier for the transaction. Retrieve valid values from `GET /v1/countries`. Must match the country in which the original payment was made. |

**Optional fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `webhook_url` | string | Endpoint to receive refund status notifications |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "message": "Refund queued",
  "data": {
    "refund_id": "ref_XXXXXX",
    "status": "pending",
    "reference": "APX-RF-XXXXXX"
  }
}
```

---

### Validation Rules
- Vesicash returns `400` if `amount` exceeds the original payment
- The `payment_reference` must map to a completed (not failed) transaction
- Multiple partial refunds are supported up to the original amount total

---

### Failure Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS` | Wallet balance insufficient to fund the refund |
| `DECLINED_TRANSACTION` | Refund rejected by card network |
| `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` | Third-party system delay — retry may be appropriate |

exec: console.log('[Vesicash] Refund', i + 1, ':', r.refund_id, '|', r.status, '| Amount:', r.amount);

request.description:
## Check Refund Status

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_reference`

Returns the full transaction record including the `refunds[]` array. Use this to confirm the refund outcome after receiving a webhook notification.

---

### Key Fields to Inspect

```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "success",
    "refunds": [
      {
        "refund_id": "ref_XXXXXX",
        "amount": 10000,
        "status": "success",
        "reason": "Customer requested cancellation",
        "reference": "APX-RF-XXXXXX",
        "created_at": "2026-05-25T11:00:00Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-05-25T11:00:30Z"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### Refund Status Values

| Value | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `pending` | Queued, not yet processed |
| `success` | Funds reversed to cardholder |
| `failed` | Reversal failed — see `failure_reason` |

request.description:
## Get All Payments for Business

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment`

Returns a paginated list of all transactions associated with your business account.

---

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | No | The page of results to retrieve. Starts at `1`. Use alongside `limit` to paginate through large result sets. |
| `limit` | integer | No | Number of transactions to return per page. Defaults to `20`. Maximum allowed value is `100`. |
| `reference` | string | No | Filter results to a single transaction by its Vesicash reference string. Returns a list containing one item when a match is found. |
| `from` | integer | No | Start of the date range as a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). Only transactions created on or after this time are returned. |
| `to` | integer | No | End of the date range as a Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch). Only transactions created on or before this time are returned. |

---

### Response
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "code": 200,
  "data": {
    "payments": [
      {
        "id": "pay_XXX",
        "amount": 10000,
        "environment": "live",
```

request.description:
## Get Full Transaction Details

**Endpoint:** `GET /v1/payment/:payment_ref`

Returns the complete transaction record, including fields specific to refunds, payouts, and fraud monitoring. Use this endpoint when you need the full audit trail.

---

### Additional Fields (vs. basic status check)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `charge` | number | Vesicash processing fee applied |
| `narration` | string | Transaction description |
| `provider_transaction_id` | string | ID assigned by MPGS / banking network |
| `operator` | string | Payment network used (e.g. `"mpgs"`) |
| `is_disbursed` | boolean | `true` when payout funds have reached beneficiary |
| `is_fraud_resolved` | boolean | `false` while fraud investigation is open |
| `refunds` | array | All refunds associated with this transaction |
| `updated_at` | string | Last status change timestamp |

---

### Use Cases
- Dispute resolution: check `is_fraud_resolved` and `refunds[]`
- Payout audit: check `is_disbursed`
- Reconciliation: compare `amount`, `charge`, `provider_transaction_id`

item.name: Webhooks
item.description:
## Webhooks

### In a nutshell
Vesicash sends real-time event notifications to your server via HTTP POST requests. Use them to drive order fulfilment, payout confirmations, and refund updates.

### Before you begin
- Configure your `webhook_url` in the Vesicash dashboard or via the Generate Webhook Secret endpoint.
- Store your webhook secret securely on your server.

### Security
Every webhook includes an `x-vesicash-signature` header. Always verify it before processing the payload.

### Retry policy
If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status, Vesicash retries with exponential backoff: 1 minute, 5 minutes, 30 minutes, 2 hours, 6 hours. After 5 failed attempts, the event is marked as undelivered.

### Response requirements
Your endpoint must return a `200 OK` response within 30 seconds.

## Webhooks Overview

### In a nutshell
Webhooks let your server receive real-time notifications when important payment events happen — no polling required.

### Before you begin
- Generate your webhook secret from the [Vesicash Dashboard](https://portal.vesicash.com) under Settings → Webhooks.
- Ensure your endpoint is publicly accessible and can receive `POST` requests.
- Respond with `200 OK` within **30 seconds** — process events asynchronously to avoid timeouts.

## Event Types

| Event | Trigger |
|-------|---------|
| `payment.completed` | Customer payment successfully processed |
| `payment.failed` | Payment attempt failed or was abandoned |
| `payout.completed` | Payout delivered to beneficiary |
| `payout.failed` | Payout could not be delivered |
| `refund.completed` | Refund successfully reversed to customer |
| `refund.failed` | Refund could not be processed |

## Retry Policy

If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status or times out, Vesicash retries with exponential backoff:

```@callout type="note" title="Retry schedule"
1 minute → 5 minutes → 30 minutes → 2 hours → 6 hours. After 5 failed attempts, the event is dropped. Monitor your endpoint health closely to avoid missed events.
```

## Signature Verification

```@callout type="warning" title="Always verify before processing"
Every webhook payload is signed with HMAC-SHA256. Never process an event without first verifying `x-vesicash-signature` against your webhook secret. See the [Verify Webhook Signature](/webhooks/verify-webhook-signature) guide.
```

## Related
- [Verify Webhook Signature](/webhooks/verify-webhook-signature)
- [payment.completed](/webhooks/payment-completed)

## Webhook: payment.completed



---

Triggered when a customer payment is fully processed and funds are available in your collection balance.

### Payload Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `event` | string | Always `"payment.completed"` |
| `data.reference` | string | Vesicash transaction reference |
| `data.merchant_reference` | string | Your custom reference (if provided at init) |
| `data.status` | string | Always `"completed"` for this event |
| `data.amount` | number | Amount collected |
| `data.currency` | string | Currency of the transaction |
| `data.net_amount` | number | Amount after Vesicash fees |

---

### Your webhook handler should:
1. Verify `x-vesicash-signature` before any processing
2. Respond with `200 OK` immediately (process async)
3. Look up the order by `data.merchant_reference`
4. Confirm `data.status === "completed"` before fulfilling the order
5. Mark the order as paid and trigger fulfillment

## Webhook: payment.failed



---

Triggered when a payment cannot be processed — due to insufficient funds, network timeout, or provider rejection.

### Key Fields

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `data.status` | Always `"failed"` |
| `data.failure_reason` | Machine-readable failure code (see Error Reference) |

### Your handler should:
1. Verify signature
2. Notify the customer of the failed payment
3. Do NOT fulfil the order
4. Optionally prompt retry

## Webhook: payout.completed



---

`is_disbursed: true` confirms funds have reached the beneficiary. Update your records accordingly.

## Webhook: payout.failed



---

Check `failure_reason` to determine the cause. Common reasons: `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND`, `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT`.

## Webhook: refund.completed



---

Update your order management system to reflect the refund. Check `original_payment_reference` to match against the original order.
